channel.title: Thorpe Family History
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Thorpe Family History

item.title: Home
item.description: Home This page is the secondary orientation page for the Thorpe family research vault. For the public starting point, begin at Thorpe Family History.

item.title: John Thorp
item.description: John Thorp Biographical Profile Name: John Thorp Dates: 1791-1850 Role in this project: Thorpe line patriarch; progenitor of William Monroe Thorp line documented in 1835-1899.

item.title: WallerHannah
item.description: WallerHannah Biographical Profile Name: WallerHannah Role in this project: Individual indexed in Census InDesign summary (2026-04-24 intake).

item.title: WHITFIELD, John
item.description: WHITFIELD, John Biographical Profile Name: WHITFIELD, John Role in this project: Individual indexed in Census InDesign summary (2026-04-24 intake).
:+,7), Biographical Profile Name: :+,7), Role in this project: Individual indexed in Census InDesign summary (2026-04-24 intake).

item.title: William Monroe Thorp
item.description: William Monroe Thorp Biographical Profile Name: William Monroe Thorp Dates: 1835-1899 Role in this project: Bridge generation Thorpe patriarch; son of John Thorp, father of Uriah Blake Thorpe and Ralph Gerald Thorpe.

item.title: Ault Tallman Branch Summary
item.description: Ault and Tallman Branch Summary This branch is a strong visitor path because it has a clear household story: Ohio Ault records, Iowa Tallman farming households, and later Soldiers’ Home and cemetery evidence.

item.title: Bangle, Beneworth, Brooks, Filkins, and Allied Families Branch Summary
item.description: Bangle, Beneworth, Brooks, Filkins, and Allied Families Branch Summary Branch Overview Time Period: 19th century (1841–1871 UK census era) Geographic Range: UK parishes, scattered US settlement (Ohio, Iowa) Primary Occupations: Agricultural workers, household service, skilled trades Key Ancestor Lin...

item.title: Baxter, Hiskey, Harrison, and How Families Branch Summary
item.description: Baxter, Hiskey, Harrison, and How Families Branch Summary Branch Overview Time Period: 19th century (1841–1871 UK census era, some US settlement) Geographic Range: UK parishes, Midlands/Southern England region; scattered Ohio settlement Primary Occupations: Agricultural workers, household service, g...

item.title: Bellamy, Kelly, Emblow, and Munson Branch Summary
item.description: Bellamy, Kelly, Emblow, and Munson Branch Summary Branch Overview Time Period: 1787–1960s (spanning 1841–1871 UK censuses and 19th-century American settlement) Geographic Range: Lincolnshire (Bourn) and Bedhouses; scattered US settlement (Wisconsin, Iowa, Ohio) Primary Occupations: Miller, baker, fa...
